questions.question: Why is terrace farming important in hilly areas?

options.A: It increases the slope of the land
options.B: It prevents soil erosion and conserves water

questions.explanation: Terracing slows water runoff and prevents soil erosion while conserving water.

questions.question: Which of the following is a human adaptation to tropical rainforest regions?
questions.explanation: Houses on stilts prevent flooding and protect from insects and moisture in rainforest regions.

questions.question: Slash-and-burn agriculture is practiced mainly in:
questions.explanation: This method clears forest land for temporary cultivation in rainforest areas.

questions.question: Which human activity contributes most to desertification?
questions.explanation: Deforestation and overuse of land degrade soil, leading to desertification.

questions.question: Which of the following methods helps conserve water in arid regions?
questions.explanation: Rainwater harvesting stores water for domestic and agricultural use in dry areas.

questions.question: Human settlements in flood-prone areas often include:
questions.explanation: Raised houses and levees protect communities from frequent flooding.

questions.question: Which of the following is a negative effect of human activity on forests?
questions.explanation: Logging, deforestation, and urbanization destroy habitats and reduce biodiversity.

questions.question: Which adaptation helps humans survive in coastal cyclone-prone areas?
questions.explanation: Cyclone shelters and embankments protect life and property from storms.

questions.question: How does industrialization affect air quality?
questions.explanation: Factories release pollutants like smoke and gases, reducing air quality.

questions.question: Which energy source can reduce dependence on fossil fuels?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is renewable and environmentally friendly.

questions.question: Overpopulation can cause which of the following environmental problems?

options.A: Overuse of water and land resources

questions.explanation: High population increases demand for natural resources, leading to environmental stress.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of human adaptation to desert regions?

options.A: Building mud houses and storing water

questions.explanation: Mud houses provide insulation and storing water helps survive arid conditions.

questions.question: Which farming practice helps maintain soil fertility in tropical forests?
questions.explanation: Allowing land to lie fallow after slash-and-burn helps restore soil fertility.

questions.question: How can human activities increase the risk of floods?

options.B: By deforestation and building on floodplains

questions.explanation: Deforestation reduces water absorption and construction on floodplains blocks natural drainage.

questions.question: Which of the following contributes to climate change?
questions.explanation: Greenhouse gas emissions from deforestation and fossil fuels increase global temperatures.

questions.question: Which adaptation is useful for people living in cold mountainous regions?

options.A: Wearing insulated clothing and building stone houses

questions.explanation: Insulated clothing and stone houses protect against cold and snow.

questions.question: Which of the following is a consequence of overgrazing?
questions.explanation: Overgrazing removes vegetation, exposing soil to erosion and degradation.

questions.question: Why is sustainable resource management important?

options.A: To ensure availability for future generations

questions.explanation: Sustainable use of resources maintains ecological balance and future supply.

questions.question: Which adaptation is used in floodplains to protect agriculture?
questions.explanation: Raised platforms and embankments prevent crop damage from floods.

questions.question: Which human activity leads to habitat fragmentation?
questions.explanation: Building cities and roads divides natural habitats and affects wildlife.

questions.question: Which adaptation is helpful in cyclone-prone coastal regions?
questions.explanation: Shelters and seawalls protect lives and property from cyclones.

questions.question: Which method helps restore soil fertility after slash-and-burn cultivation?
questions.explanation: Leaving land fallow allows soil nutrients to regenerate.

questions.question: Why do humans adopt irrigation in plains?

options.A: To supply water for crops during dry seasons

questions.explanation: Irrigation ensures crop growth when rainfall is insufficient.

questions.question: Which human activity increases greenhouse gas emissions?
questions.explanation: Cutting forests and burning fossil fuels release carbon dioxide and other gases.

questions.question: Which adaptation helps communities in mountainous cold regions conserve heat?
questions.explanation: Thick houses and clothing retain heat and protect from cold winds.
